text.title: Django Managers and QuerySets
text.summary: Custom QuerySet, Manager.from_queryset(), chainable methods, and factory methods.

rule.statement: Define domain-specific filter logic as QuerySet methods, not in service functions or views. Use Manager.from_queryset(QuerySet)() — note the trailing () to instantiate.
rule.rationale: QuerySet methods are chainable; service-level filtering is not. Missing () on from_queryset creates the class object, not an instance — a confusing runtime error.

rule.statement: If a child model redefines objects, it must replicate the alive() filter or the soft-delete protection is lost.
rule.rationale: Custom managers override the default; naively replacing objects loses the alive filter and returns deleted rows.
